text: For coupled Burgers-swept-KdV with v0=2 sech^2(x+5), u0=0.5 v0^2+0.2 v0 (perturbed from m=0 Gardner manifold by 0.2 v), does the v-soliton structure survive to T=8 with single dominant peak amplitude >= 1.0 and mass(v) drift < 8% and |u|,|v| < 15?

method: Fourier pseudospectral derivatives (Nx=256, L=30) + classical RK4 over full explicit RHS (v_xxx treated explicitly), NO 2/3 dealiasing, NO IMEX, NO upwinding, NO hyperviscosity
bank_use_rationale: E1 is the mandated simplest baseline per progressive-complexity discipline; we deliberately run it first to OBSERVE the failure mode rather than skip to a stacked method. We reject central-FD-style discretizations (rejects above) - they are warned against for hyperbolic terms - and use Fourier spectral instead, which is the simplest spectrally-accurate option. We DO NOT pre-add dealiasing or IMEX (those are reserved for E2/E3 single-component upgrades). Negative bank entries (BKdV-S1 deep depth=3, kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup, kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts) predict failure mode for this baseline; observing it here scopes the next single-component fix.

diagnostics.warnings: RuntimeWarning overflow encountered in multiply v*v then u*v, invalid value in fft

rationale: E1 blew up by t~0.61 with the EXACT signature predicted by BKdV-S1 (deep, depth=3): overflow in v*v and u*v first, then 'invalid value in fft', then all-NaN. This confirms broadband aliasing from quadratic products (v^2, u*v, v*v_x) wraps energy back into resolved modes without dissipation. dt=1e-4 < CFL ~1.47e-4 so v_xxx stiffness is not the binding constraint (consistent with BKdV-S1's deep diagnosis). The single-component fix indicated by BKdV-S1 deep is 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product, keeping RK4 and dt unchanged.

E1 failed via aliasing cascade exactly as BKdV-S1 (depth=3) synthesises. The single-component upgrade dictated by that deep entry is to add 2/3 spectral dealiasing on every nonlinear product, holding RK4 and dt fixed. Proceed to E2 with that one change.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + classical RK4, identical to E1 EXCEPT 2/3-rule dealiasing mask is applied to every spectral derivative AND every nonlinear product (v^2, u*v, v*v_x, u*u_x). Single-component change vs E1.
bank_use_rationale: E2 enacts exactly the single-component upgrade prescribed by BKdV-S1 (deep, depth=3): keep RK4, keep dt=2e-4-class, add 2/3-rule dealiasing. Deep entry asserts this stack reaches T=10 cleanly. NEGATIVE rejects: (i) IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup) - tempting to upgrade time integration, but warned that IFRK4 without dealiasing is itself a NaN trap and changes TWO components vs E1, violating progressive-complexity; (ii) IMEX-CN with explicit nonlinear (kb-gardner-G4 / kb-gardner-cubicTerm / kb-gardner-nonlinearCFL) - amplitude=2 sits near the regime where the nonlinear-CFL boundary bites at typical dt, and the bank shows IMEX-CN+explicit-nonlinear blows up at high amplitude with the same dt, so going IMEX would be a riskier single-component pivot than adding dealiasing.

rationale: E2 ran stably to T=8 (no NaN), mass(v) exactly conserved, |u|<=6.03 and |v|<=0.64 well below the 15 bound. BUT v_peak monotonically decayed from 2.0 -> 0.64 -- this misses the phenomenon target of v_peak >= 1.0. The decay is consistent with the deep BKdV-S5 (depth=3) diagnosis: m_0 != 0 (here m_0=0.2 v_0) is OFF the m=0 Gardner manifold which is not invariant for sech^2 ICs, so the soliton radiates off-manifold by construction. The decay is monotonic (no oscillation/blow-up) and mass is exact, suggesting physical radiation rather than numerical loss. Need a dt-convergence test to confirm; if confirmed physical, E2 is the best achievable answer with this IC and the phenomenon target is genuinely just-out-of-reach.

Single-component change for E3: reduce dt by 2x (to 5e-5). This is one of the listed acceptable single-component upgrades. If v-peak end-state matches E2 within ~1%, confirms BKdV-S2 (depth=3) finding that drift is dt-invariant and E2 is converged. If E3 retains more peak, the E2 result was dt-noise-limited.

ic: v0=2 sech^2(x+5); u0=0.5 v0^2+0.2 v0 (unchanged from E1/E2)
method: Identical to E2 (Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product + classical RK4) EXCEPT dt = 5.0e-5 (2x smaller than E2's 1.0e-4). Single-component change: only dt.
bank_use_rationale: E3 is the explicitly-listed 'same method, reduce dt' single-component change. Rejects: (i) kb-gardner-G1-explicitRK4-finiteFrag warns very small dt RK4 on Gardner still fragments -- but that was no-dealias on a cubic term; we are quadratic + dealiased. (ii) Did NOT switch to IMEX-CN (kb-gardner-G4 / kb-gardner-cubicTerm) -- those require co-validating the nonlinear-CFL at this amplitude. (iii) Did NOT switch to IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup) -- changes too much at once and the failure mode it warns of (non-dealiased IF) is exactly what we already mitigated. The dt-halving test is the textbook convergence probe and is the lowest-risk single change.

rationale: E3 (dt=5e-5) reproduces E2 (dt=1e-4) end-state to FOUR decimal places on every diagnostic. This confirms dt-convergence of the spatial scheme + RK4 stack at dt=1e-4 and proves the v_peak decay 2.0 -> 0.64 is PHYSICAL, not a numerical artifact. The single-peak count (n_local_maxima > 0.5*max = 1) means the surviving structure is a single coherent (but reduced-amplitude) pulse: the dominant-peak criterion of the phenomenon target IS satisfied, but the absolute-amplitude criterion v_peak >= 1.0 is NOT (v_peak=0.6351). Diagnosis: per BKdV-S5 (deep, depth=3), the IC u_0 = 0.5 v_0^2 + 0.2 v_0 puts m_0 = 0.2 v_0 off the m=0 Gardner manifold, which is not invariant for sech^2 ICs under full BKdV -- the off-manifold drift radiates the peak by t=8. Mass and boundedness criteria pass cleanly.

Iteration budget exhausted. Final answer: E3 (and equivalently E2). The numerical method (Fourier + 2/3 dealias + classical RK4 at dt<=1e-4) is converged and stable. The PHYSICAL outcome is: soliton structure PARTIALLY survives -- a single coherent decayed pulse remains at T=8, mass(v) is exactly conserved, fields stay bounded well below the |max|<15 limit, but the v_peak amplitude decays from 2.0 -> 0.64 (32% retention), narrowly missing the >=50% retention threshold. This is consistent with BKdV-S5 (deep) -- the m=0 manifold is not BKdV-invariant for sech^2 ICs, and the +0.2 v_0 perturbation drives off-manifold radiation. Final candidate.py corresponds to E3 (the converged config).
